body:
Q1 work well
Q2 only for small inputs
explanation: The provided code consists of two separate problems. The first, implemented in 'Hila_ChocolateA.cpp', aims to determine the minimum number of cuts required to divide an n x m chocolate bar into 1x1 pieces. It utilizes dynamic programming to find the optimal solution based on horizontal and vertical cuts. The second part, in 'HilaQueens.cpp', solves the N-Queens problem using a backtracking algorithm. It places queens on an N x N chessboard while ensuring that no two queens can attack each other, providing a solution when possible or indicating that no solution exists for certain values of N.
name: 2010-05-16_hila-16-5-2010
subject: Hila 16.5.2010
summary: This programming assignment includes solutions to the N-Queens problem and a dynamic programming problem involving cuts on a chocolate bar.
